sn35.231:4.1: "Suppose, bhikkhus, there was a milk-sap tree[n.167] Khīrarukkha: a tree that exudes a milky sap. The four are types of fig trees; see too SN46.39. —an assattha or a banyan or a pilakkha or an udumbara-fresh, young, tender. If a man breaks it here and there with a sharp axe, would sap come out?"
sn35.231:4.2: "Yes, venerable sir. For what reason? Because there is sap."
